name: HTTP/3
description: HTTP/3 is the third major version of the Hypertext Transfer Protocol, using QUIC (Quick UDP Internet Connections) as its transport layer instead of TCP. It reduces latency through faster connection establishment, improved multiplexing, and better performance on lossy networks.
advantages: No head-of-line blocking compared to HTTP/2 over TCP
Better performance on unreliable or high-latency networks
Built-in encryption with TLS 1.3 by default
Automatic connection migration across network changes
disadvantages: Requires both server and client support
Some network appliances and firewalls may block UDP
Not all CDN providers have full HTTP/3 support
CPU overhead from QUIC encryption on servers
